preamble: The shift from the voiceless glottal fricative [h] to the voiceless velar fricative [x] is a process of fortition known as buccalization. It involves moving the place of articulation from the larynx forward into the oral cavity, where the tongue dorsum creates a narrow constriction against the soft palate.

examples.note: Original /x/ weakened to [h] in most positions, but can undergo emphatic re-strengthening back to [x] or [χ].
Theorized intermediate stage in the fortition of PIE *-s- to aspirated stops in Classical Armenian.

sources: Ohala, J. J. (1983). The origin of sound patterns in vocal tract constraints.
Hock, H. H. (1991). Principles of Historical Linguistics.
